name: Steve Turre
abstract: Stephen Johnson Turre (born September 12, 1948, in Omaha, Nebraska) is an American jazz trombonist and a pioneer of using seashells as instruments, a composer, arranger, and educator at the collegiate-conservatory level. For sixty-two years, Turre has been active in jazz, rock, and Latin jazz – in live venues, recording studios, televisi…

about_quotes.speaker: Terri Lyne Carrington
about_quotes.evidence: I used to see Steve every so often... he always reminds me about when I would sit in back in those days when I was eleven, ten, eleven, twelve.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] So you had Virgil Jones, Steve Turre, Frank Lacy, John Clark, so again, truly a big band... [INTERVIEWEE] The older guys.

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: Steve Turre has picked that up. Every time you see him, it’s just “Bam!”
